44	if (process.env.NEXT_RUNTIME !== 'edge') {

58	/**
59	 * The AppPageModule is the type of the module exported by the bundled app page
60	 * module.
61	 */
62	export type AppPageModule = typeof import('../../../build/templates/app-page')
63	
64	type AppPageUserlandModule = {
65	  /**
66	   * The tree created in next-app-loader that holds component segments and modules
67	   */
68	  loaderTree: LoaderTree
69	}
70	
71	export interface AppPageRouteHandlerContext extends RouteModuleHandleContext {
72	  page: string
73	  query: NextParsedUrlQuery
74	  fallbackRouteParams: OpaqueFallbackRouteParams | null
75	  renderOpts: RenderOpts
76	  serverComponentsHmrCache?: ServerComponentsHmrCache
77	  sharedContext: AppSharedContext
78	}
79	
80	export type AppPageRouteModuleOptions = RouteModuleOptions<
81	  AppPageRouteDefinition,
82	  AppPageUserlandModule
83	>
84	
85	export class AppPageRouteModule extends RouteModule<
86	  AppPageRouteDefinition,
87	  AppPageUserlandModule
88	> {
89	  private matchers = new WeakMap<
90	    DeepReadonly<PrerenderManifest>,
91	    PrerenderManifestMatcher
92	  >()
93	  public match(
94	    pathname: string,
95	    prerenderManifest: DeepReadonly<PrerenderManifest>
96	  ) {
97	    // Lazily create the matcher based on the provided prerender manifest.
98	    let matcher = this.matchers.get(prerenderManifest)
99	    if (!matcher) {
100	      matcher = new PrerenderManifestMatcher(
101	        this.definition.pathname,
102	        prerenderManifest
103	      )
104	      this.matchers.set(prerenderManifest, matcher)
105	    }
106	
107	    // Match the pathname to the dynamic route.
108	    return matcher.match(pathname)
109	  }
110	
111	  private normalizers = {
112	    rsc: new RSCPathnameNormalizer(),
113	    segmentPrefetchRSC: new SegmentPrefixRSCPathnameNormalizer(),
114	  }
115	
116	  public normalizeUrl(
117	    req: IncomingMessage | BaseNextRequest,
118	    parsedUrl: UrlWithParsedQuery
119	  ) {
120	    if (this.normalizers.segmentPrefetchRSC.match(parsedUrl.pathname || '/')) {
121	      const result = this.normalizers.segmentPrefetchRSC.extract(
122	        parsedUrl.pathname || '/'
123	      )
124	      if (!result) return false
125	
126	      const { originalPathname, segmentPath } = result
127	      parsedUrl.pathname = originalPathname
128	
129	      // Mark the request as a router prefetch request.
130	      req.headers[RSC_HEADER] = '1'
131	      req.headers[NEXT_ROUTER_PREFETCH_HEADER] = '1'
132	      req.headers[NEXT_ROUTER_SEGMENT_PREFETCH_HEADER] = segmentPath
133	
134	      addRequestMeta(req, 'isRSCRequest', true)
135	      addRequestMeta(req, 'isPrefetchRSCRequest', true)
136	      addRequestMeta(req, 'segmentPrefetchRSCRequest', segmentPath)
137	    } else if (this.normalizers.rsc.match(parsedUrl.pathname || '/')) {
138	      parsedUrl.pathname = this.normalizers.rsc.normalize(
139	        parsedUrl.pathname || '/',
140	        true
141	      )
142	
143	      // Mark the request as a RSC request.
144	      req.headers[RSC_HEADER] = '1'
145	    } else {
146	      super.normalizeUrl(req, parsedUrl)
147	    }
148	
149	    // Minimal adapters can bypass base-server request normalization and invoke
150	    // route modules directly, so derive RSC/prefetch metadata from headers.
151	    applyAppPageRscRequestMetaFromHeaders(req)
152	    normalizeAppPageRequestUrl(req, parsedUrl.pathname || '/')
153	  }
154	
155	  public render(
156	    req: BaseNextRequest,
157	    res: BaseNextResponse,
158	    context: AppPageRouteHandlerContext
159	  ): Promise<RenderResult> {
160	    return renderToHTMLOrFlight(
161	      req,
162	      res,
163	      context.page,
164	      context.query,
165	      context.fallbackRouteParams,
166	      context.renderOpts,
167	      context.serverComponentsHmrCache,
168	      context.sharedContext
169	    )
170	  }
171	
172	  private pathCouldBeIntercepted(
173	    resolvedPathname: string,
174	    interceptionRoutePatterns: RegExp[]
175	  ): boolean {
176	    return (
177	      isInterceptionRouteAppPath(resolvedPathname) ||
178	      interceptionRoutePatterns.some((regexp) => {
179	        return regexp.test(resolvedPathname)
180	      })
181	    )
182	  }
183	
184	  public getVaryHeader(
185	    resolvedPathname: string,
186	    interceptionRoutePatterns: RegExp[]
187	  ): string {
188	    const baseVaryHeader = `${RSC_HEADER}, ${NEXT_ROUTER_STATE_TREE_HEADER}, ${NEXT_ROUTER_PREFETCH_HEADER}, ${NEXT_ROUTER_SEGMENT_PREFETCH_HEADER}`
189	
190	    if (
191	      this.pathCouldBeIntercepted(resolvedPathname, interceptionRoutePatterns)
192	    ) {
193	      // Interception route responses can vary based on the `Next-URL` header.
194	      // We use the Vary header to signal this behavior to the client to properly cache the response.
195	      return `${baseVaryHeader}, ${NEXT_URL}`
196	    } else {
197	      // We don't need to include `Next-URL` in the Vary header for non-interception routes since it won't affect the response.
198	      // We also set this header for pages to avoid caching issues when navigating between pages and app.
199	      return baseVaryHeader
200	    }

1865	  protected getWaitUntil(): WaitUntil | undefined {
1866	    const builtinRequestContext = getBuiltinRequestContext()
1867	    if (builtinRequestContext) {
1868	      // the platform provided a request context.
1869	      // use the `waitUntil` from there, whether actually present or not --
1870	      // if not present, `after` will error.
1871	
1872	      // NOTE: if we're in an edge runtime sandbox, this context will be used to forward the outer waitUntil.
1873	      return builtinRequestContext.waitUntil
1874	    }
1875	
1876	    if (this.minimalMode) {
1877	      // we're built for a serverless environment, and `waitUntil` is not available,
1878	      // but using a noop would likely lead to incorrect behavior,
1879	      // because we have no way of keeping the invocation alive.
1880	      // return nothing, and `after` will error if used.
1881	      //
1882	      // NOTE: for edge functions, `NextWebServer` always runs in minimal mode.
1883	      //
1884	      // NOTE: if we're in an edge runtime sandbox, waitUntil will be passed in using "@next/request-context",
1885	      // so we won't get here.

1896	  private async renderImpl(
1897	    req: ServerRequest,
1898	    res: ServerResponse,
1899	    pathname: string,
1900	    query: NextParsedUrlQuery = {},
1901	    parsedUrl?: NextUrlWithParsedQuery,
1902	    internalRender = false
1903	  ): Promise<void> {
1904	    if (!pathname.startsWith('/')) {
1905	      console.warn(
1906	        `Cannot render page with path "${pathname}", did you mean "/${pathname}"?. See more info here: https://nextjs.org/docs/messages/render-no-starting-slash`
1907	      )
1908	    }
1909	
1910	    if (
1911	      this.serverOptions.customServer &&
1912	      pathname === '/index' &&
1913	      !(await this.hasPage('/index'))
1914	    ) {
1915	      // maintain backwards compatibility for custom server
1916	      // (see custom-server integration tests)
1917	      pathname = '/'
1918	    }
1919	
1920	    const ua = req.headers['user-agent'] || ''
1921	    this.renderOpts.botType = getBotType(ua)
1922	
1923	    // we allow custom servers to call render for all URLs
1924	    // so check if we need to serve a static _next file or not.
1925	    // we don't modify the URL for _next/data request but still
1926	    // call render so we special case this to prevent an infinite loop
1927	    if (
1928	      !internalRender &&
1929	      !this.minimalMode &&
1930	      !getRequestMeta(req, 'isNextDataReq') &&
1931	      (req.url?.match(/^\/_next\//) ||
1932	        (this.hasStaticDir && req.url!.match(/^\/static\//)))
1933	    ) {
1934	      return this.handleRequest(req, res, parsedUrl)
1935	    }
1936	
1937	    if (isBlockedPage(pathname)) {
1938	      return this.render404(req, res, parsedUrl)
1939	    }
1940	
1941	    return this.pipe((ctx) => this.renderToResponse(ctx), {
1942	      req,
1943	      res,
1944	      pathname,
1945	      query,
1946	    })
1947	  }

1977	  ): Promise<ResponsePayload | null> {

1991	      this.interceptionRoutePatterns.some((regexp) => {

1997	  protected setVaryHeader(
1998	    req: ServerRequest,
1999	    res: ServerResponse,
2000	    isAppPath: boolean,
2001	    resolvedPathname: string
2002	  ): void {
2003	    const baseVaryHeader = `${RSC_HEADER}, ${NEXT_ROUTER_STATE_TREE_HEADER}, ${NEXT_ROUTER_PREFETCH_HEADER}, ${NEXT_ROUTER_SEGMENT_PREFETCH_HEADER}`
2004	    const isRSCRequest = getRequestMeta(req, 'isRSCRequest') ?? false
2005	
2006	    let addedNextUrlToVary = false
2007	
2008	    if (isAppPath && this.pathCouldBeIntercepted(resolvedPathname)) {
2009	      // Interception route responses can vary based on the `Next-URL` header.
2010	      // We use the Vary header to signal this behavior to the client to properly cache the response.
2011	      res.appendHeader('vary', `${baseVaryHeader}, ${NEXT_URL}`)
2012	      addedNextUrlToVary = true
2013	    } else if (isAppPath || isRSCRequest) {
2014	      // We don't need to include `Next-URL` in the Vary header for non-interception routes since it won't affect the response.
2015	      // We also set this header for pages to avoid caching issues when navigating between pages and app.
2016	      res.appendHeader('vary', baseVaryHeader)
2017	    }
2018	
2019	    if (!addedNextUrlToVary) {
2020	      // Remove `Next-URL` from the request headers we determined it wasn't necessary to include in the Vary header.
2021	      // This is to avoid any dependency on the `Next-URL` header being present when preparing the response.
2022	      delete req.headers[NEXT_URL]

2026	  private async renderToResponseWithComponentsImpl(
2027	    {
2028	      req,
2029	      res,
2030	      pathname,
2031	      renderOpts: opts,
2032	    }: RequestContext<ServerRequest, ServerResponse>,
2033	    { components, query }: FindComponentsResult

2042	      pathname === '/500' || (isErrorPathname && res.statusCode === 500)

export default abstract class Server<
  ServerOptions extends Options = Options,
  ServerRequest extends BaseNextRequest = BaseNextRequest,
  ServerResponse extends BaseNextResponse = BaseNextResponse,
> {
  public readonly hostname?: string
  public readonly fetchHostname?: string
  public readonly port?: number
  protected readonly dir: string
  protected readonly quiet: boolean
  protected readonly nextConfig: NextConfigRuntime
  protected readonly distDir: string
  protected readonly publicDir: string
  protected readonly hasStaticDir: boolean
  protected readonly pagesManifest?: PagesManifest
  protected readonly appPathsManifest?: PagesManifest
  protected readonly buildId: string
  protected readonly deploymentId: string
  protected readonly dev: boolean
  protected readonly minimalMode: boolean
  protected readonly renderOpts: BaseRenderOpts
  protected readonly serverOptions: Readonly<ServerOptions>
  protected readonly appPathRoutes?: Record<string, string[]>
  protected readonly clientReferenceManifest?: DeepReadonly<ClientReferenceManifest>
  protected interceptionRoutePatterns: RegExp[]
  protected nextFontManifest?: DeepReadonly<NextFontManifest>
  protected instrumentation: InstrumentationModule | undefined

  protected abstract getPublicDir(): string
  protected abstract getHasStaticDir(): boolean
  protected abstract getPagesManifest(): PagesManifest | undefined
  protected abstract getAppPathsManifest(): PagesManifest | undefined
  protected abstract getBuildId(): string
  protected abstract getinterceptionRoutePatterns(): RegExp[]

  protected readonly enabledDirectories: NextEnabledDirectories
  protected abstract getEnabledDirectories(dev: boolean): NextEnabledDirectories

  protected readonly experimentalTestProxy?: boolean

  protected abstract findPageComponents(params: {
    locale: string | undefined
    page: string
    query: NextParsedUrlQuery
    params: Params
    isAppPath: boolean
    // The following parameters are used in the development server's
    // implementation.
    sriEnabled?: boolean
    appPaths?: ReadonlyArray<string> | null
    shouldEnsure?: boolean
    url?: string
  }): Promise<FindComponentsResult | null>
  protected abstract getPrerenderManifest(): DeepReadonly<PrerenderManifest>
  protected abstract getNextFontManifest():
    | DeepReadonly<NextFontManifest>
    | undefined
  protected abstract attachRequestMeta(
    req: ServerRequest,
    parsedUrl: NextUrlWithParsedQuery
  ): void
  protected abstract hasPage(pathname: string): Promise<boolean>

  protected abstract sendRenderResult(
    req: ServerRequest,
    res: ServerResponse,
    options: {
      result: RenderResult
      generateEtags: boolean
      poweredByHeader: boolean
      cacheControl: CacheControl | undefined
    }
  ): Promise<void>

  protected abstract runApi(
    req: ServerRequest,
    res: ServerResponse,
    query: ParsedUrlQuery,
    match: PagesAPIRouteMatch
  ): Promise<boolean>

1	import type { IncomingMessage, ServerResponse } from 'http'
2	import type { ParsedUrlQuery } from 'querystring'
3	import type { UrlWithParsedQuery } from 'url'
4	import type { BaseNextRequest } from './base-http'
5	import type { CloneableBody } from './body-streams'
6	import type { RouteMatch } from './route-matches/route-match'
7	import type { NEXT_RSC_UNION_QUERY } from '../client/components/app-router-headers'
8	import type {
9	  ResponseCacheEntry,
10	  ServerComponentsHmrCache,
11	} from './response-cache'
12	import type { PagesDevOverlayBridgeType } from '../next-devtools/userspace/pages/pages-dev-overlay-setup'
13	import type { OpaqueFallbackRouteParams } from './request/fallback-params'
14	import type { IncrementalCache } from './lib/incremental-cache'
15	import type { RevalidateFn } from './lib/router-utils/router-server-context'
16	import type { NextRequest } from './web/exports'
17	
18	// FIXME: (wyattjoh) this is a temporary solution to allow us to pass data between bundled modules
19	export const NEXT_REQUEST_META = Symbol.for('NextInternalRequestMeta')
20	
21	export type NextIncomingMessage = (
22	  | BaseNextRequest
23	  | IncomingMessage
24	  | NextRequest
25	) & {
26	  [NEXT_REQUEST_META]?: RequestMeta
27	}
28	
29	/**
30	 * The callback function to call when a response cache entry was generated or
31	 * looked up in the cache. When it returns true, the server assumes that the
32	 * handler has already responded to the request and will not do so itself.
33	 */
34	export type OnCacheEntryHandler = (
35	  /**
36	   * The response cache entry that was generated or looked up in the cache.
37	   */
38	  cacheEntry: ResponseCacheEntry,
39	
40	  /**
41	   * The request metadata.
42	   */
43	  requestMeta: {
44	    /**
45	     * The URL that was used to make the request.
46	     */
47	    url: string | undefined
48	  }
49	) => Promise<boolean | void> | boolean | void
50	
51	export interface RequestMeta {
52	  /**
53	   * The query that was used to make the request.
54	   */
55	  initQuery?: ParsedUrlQuery
56	
57	  /**
58	   * The URL that was used to make the request.
59	   */
60	  initURL?: string
61	
62	  /**
63	   * The protocol that was used to make the request.
64	   */
65	  initProtocol?: string
66	
67	  /**
68	   * The body that was read from the request. This is used to allow the body to
69	   * be read multiple times.
70	   */
71	  clonableBody?: CloneableBody
72	
73	  /**
74	   * True when the request matched a locale domain that was configured in the
75	   * next.config.js file.
76	   */
77	  isLocaleDomain?: boolean
78	
79	  /**
80	   * True when the request had locale information stripped from the pathname
81	   * part of the URL.
82	   */
83	  didStripLocale?: boolean
84	
85	  /**
86	   * If the request had its URL rewritten, this is the pathname it was rewritten
87	   * to (not a full URL, just the pathname).
88	   */
89	  rewrittenPathname?: string
90	
91	  /**
92	   * The resolved pathname for the request. Dynamic route params are
93	   * interpolated, the pathname is decoded, and the trailing slash is removed.
94	   */
95	  resolvedPathname?: string
96	
97	  /**
98	   * The cookies that were added by middleware and were added to the response.
99	   */
100	  middlewareCookie?: string[]
101	
102	  /**
103	   * The match on the request for a given route.
104	   */
105	  match?: RouteMatch
106	
107	  /**
108	   * The incremental cache to use for the request.
109	   */
110	  incrementalCache?: IncrementalCache
111	
112	  /**
113	   * The server components HMR cache, only for dev.
114	   */
115	  serverComponentsHmrCache?: ServerComponentsHmrCache
116	
117	  /**
118	   * Equals the segment path that was used for the prefetch RSC request.
119	   */
120	  segmentPrefetchRSCRequest?: string
121	
122	  /**
123	   * True when the request is for the prefetch flight data.
124	   */
125	  isPrefetchRSCRequest?: true
126	
127	  /**
128	   * True when the request is for the flight data.
129	   */
130	  isRSCRequest?: true
131	
132	  /**
133	   * A search param set by the Next.js client when performing RSC requests.
134	   * Because some CDNs do not vary their cache entries on our custom headers,
135	   * this search param represents a hash of the header values. For any cached
136	   * RSC request, we should verify that the hash matches before responding.
137	   * Otherwise this can lead to cache poisoning.
138	   * TODO: Consider not using custom request headers at all, and instead encode
139	   * everything into the search param.
140	   */
141	  cacheBustingSearchParam?: string
142	
143	  /**
144	   * True when the request is for the `/_next/data` route using the pages
145	   * router.
146	   */
147	  isNextDataReq?: true
148	
149	  /**
150	   * Postponed state to use for resumption. If present it's assumed that the
151	   * request is for a page that has postponed (there are no guarantees that the
152	   * page actually has postponed though as it would incur an additional cache
153	   * lookup).
154	   */
155	  postponed?: string
156	
157	  /**
158	   * The action body extracted from a server action request when the postponed
159	   * state was prepended to the body by the proxy. This allows the action
160	   * handler to read the action payload without re-reading the consumed stream.
161	   */
162	  actionBody?: Buffer
163	
164	  /**
165	   * If provided, this will be called when a response cache entry was generated
166	   * or looked up in the cache.
167	   *
168	   * @deprecated Use `onCacheEntryV2` instead.
169	   */
170	  onCacheEntry?: OnCacheEntryHandler
171	
172	  /**
173	   * If provided, this will be called when a response cache entry was generated
174	   * or looked up in the cache.

import type { IncomingMessage, ServerResponse } from 'http'
import type { ParsedUrlQuery } from 'querystring'
import type { UrlWithParsedQuery } from 'url'
import type { BaseNextRequest } from './base-http'
import type { CloneableBody } from './body-streams'
import type { RouteMatch } from './route-matches/route-match'
import type { NEXT_RSC_UNION_QUERY } from '../client/components/app-router-headers'
import type {
  ResponseCacheEntry,
  ServerComponentsHmrCache,
} from './response-cache'
import type { PagesDevOverlayBridgeType } from '../next-devtools/userspace/pages/pages-dev-overlay-setup'
import type { OpaqueFallbackRouteParams } from './request/fallback-params'
import type { IncrementalCache } from './lib/incremental-cache'
import type { RevalidateFn } from './lib/router-utils/router-server-context'
import type { NextRequest } from './web/exports'

// FIXME: (wyattjoh) this is a temporary solution to allow us to pass data between bundled modules
export const NEXT_REQUEST_META = Symbol.for('NextInternalRequestMeta')

export type NextIncomingMessage = (
  | BaseNextRequest
  | IncomingMessage
  | NextRequest
) & {
  [NEXT_REQUEST_META]?: RequestMeta
}

/**
 * The callback function to call when a response cache entry was generated or
 * looked up in the cache. When it returns true, the server assumes that the
 * handler has already responded to the request and will not do so itself.
 */
export type OnCacheEntryHandler = (
  /**
   * The response cache entry that was generated or looked up in the cache.
   */
  cacheEntry: ResponseCacheEntry,

  /**
   * The request metadata.
   */
  requestMeta: {
    /**
     * The URL that was used to make the request.
     */
    url: string | undefined
  }
) => Promise<boolean | void> | boolean | void

export interface RequestMeta {
  /**
   * The query that was used to make the request.
   */
  initQuery?: ParsedUrlQuery

  /**
   * The URL that was used to make the request.
   */
  initURL?: string

  /**
   * The protocol that was used to make the request.
   */
  initProtocol?: string

  /**
   * The body that was read from the request. This is used to allow the body to
   * be read multiple times.
   */
  clonableBody?: CloneableBody

  /**
   * True when the request matched a locale domain that was configured in the
   * next.config.js file.
   */
  isLocaleDomain?: boolean

  /**
   * True when the request had locale information stripped from the pathname
   * part of the URL.
   */
  didStripLocale?: boolean

  /**
   * If the request had its URL rewritten, this is the pathname it was rewritten
   * to (not a full URL, just the pathname).
   */
  rewrittenPathname?: string

  /**
   * The resolved pathname for the request. Dynamic route params are
   * interpolated, the pathname is decoded, and the trailing slash is removed.
   */
  resolvedPathname?: string

  /**
   * The cookies that were added by middleware and were added to the response.
   */
  middlewareCookie?: string[]

  /**
   * The match on the request for a given route.
   */
  match?: RouteMatch

  /**
   * The incremental cache to use for the request.
   */
  incrementalCache?: IncrementalCache

  /**
   * The server components HMR cache, only for dev.
   */
  serverComponentsHmrCache?: ServerComponentsHmrCache

  /**
   * Equals the segment path that was used for the prefetch RSC request.
   */
  segmentPrefetchRSCRequest?: string

  /**
   * True when the request is for the prefetch flight data.
   */
  isPrefetchRSCRequest?: true

  /**
   * True when the request is for the flight data.
   */
  isRSCRequest?: true

  /**
   * A search param set by the Next.js client when performing RSC requests.
   * Because some CDNs do not vary their cache entries on our custom headers,
   * this search param represents a hash of the header values. For any cached
   * RSC request, we should verify that the hash matches before responding.
   * Otherwise this can lead to cache poisoning.
   * TODO: Consider not using custom request headers at all, and instead encode
   * everything into the search param.
   */
  cacheBustingSearchParam?: string

  /**
   * True when the request is for the `/_next/data` route using the pages
   * router.
   */
  isNextDataReq?: true

  /**
   * Postponed state to use for resumption. If present it's assumed that the
   * request is for a page that has postponed (there are no guarantees that the
   * page actually has postponed though as it would incur an additional cache
   * lookup).
   */
  postponed?: string

  /**
   * The action body extracted from a server action request when the postponed
   * state was prepended to the body by the proxy. This allows the action
   * handler to read the action payload without re-reading the consumed stream.
   */
  actionBody?: Buffer

  /**
   * If provided, this will be called when a response cache entry was generated
   * or looked up in the cache.
   *
   * @deprecated Use `onCacheEntryV2` instead.
   */
  onCacheEntry?: OnCacheEntryHandler

  /**
   * If provided, this will be called when a response cache entry was generated
   * or looked up in the cache.
   */
  onCacheEntryV2?: OnCacheEntryHandler

  /**
   * The previous revalidate before rendering 404 page for notFound: true
   */
  notFoundRevalidate?: number | false

  /**
   * In development, the original source page that returned a 404.
   */
  developmentNotFoundSourcePage?: string

  /**
   * The path we routed to and should be invoked
   */
  invokePath?: string

  /**
   * The specific page output we should be matching
   */
  invokeOutput?: string

  /**
   * The status we are invoking the request with from routing
   */
